result:
I think I'd want to dramatically improve how people understand and empathize with each other across differences - whether cultural, political, ideological, or personal.

So much suffering seems to stem from our tendency to see others as fundamentally different or threatening when we disagree, rather than recognizing our shared humanity. If people could better appreciate different perspectives while still holding their own convictions, I imagine we'd see less conflict, more collaborative problem-solving, and greater compassion.

This isn't about eliminating disagreement - differences of opinion and values can be valuable. But if we could disagree while still truly seeing and caring about each other as people, I think many other problems might become more solvable.

What about you - if you could change one thing about the world, what would it be?

If people could better appreciate different perspectives while still holding their own convictions, I imagine we'd see less conflict, more collaborative problem-solving, and greater compassion.

This isn't about eliminating disagreement - differences of opinion and values can be valuable. But if we could disagree while still truly seeing and caring about each other as people, I think many other problems might become more solvable.

What about you - if you could change one thing about the world, what would it be?

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
